title: Hideaki Anno interview Animeland 32
wikitext:
[]: Hideaki Anno interview: Animeland #32             Quote Post by gwern » Sun Oct 23, 2011 12:36 pm http://eva.onegeek.org/pipermail/evangelion/2011-October/007094.html

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Oct 23, 2011 12:49 pm Not too much new stuff in there, but good interview, anyway. His thoughts on censorship are kind of interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Oct 23, 2011 12:57 pm Some interesting comments. AL: Yet, there are some fans that no longer go out with real girls... HA: It is true that some fans of animation display unfortunate behavior. AL: And yet you continue to create this kind of characters for them. HA: You need to understand that Japanese animation is an industry that is, for the most part, male, and as is quite evident, everything is made for their gratification. Further, it is more gratifying for us to draw this sort of character, rather than old grandmothers.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sun Oct 23, 2011 5:59 pm HA: Actually, I think that some censorship is necessary, but it is not normal that we should be ordered by a conventional [_bien-pensant_; see <http>] minority. I do not think you can get away with anything for the so-called well-being and protection of children.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Sun Oct 23, 2011 7:47 pm Stryker wrote: This should end any more discussion about how the Christian signs and all of such are a representation of a religion. At least, for the most part.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Oct 23, 2011 8:30 pm gatotsu911 wrote: Kind of? It's not exactly anything new. Also, the debate about whether Eva has anything to say about religion itself (not specifically Christianity/Judaism) isn't going anywhere any time soon.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sun Oct 23, 2011 9:41 pm anno knows about as much of western religion as he does of psychology, he may have done some light reading on it but in the end it's just something he used to make it look different. Eva to me seems to be more about humanism then anything else.

[]: VIKINGS FTW! BD             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Oct 24, 2011 3:10 am Hideaki Anno wrote: Japan has not known a war in nearly two generations, which is to say that we have more and more strong women, and men who become weaker over time.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Oct 29, 2011 12:21 pm In other words, he's just a geek, too.
